     The Numbers Business Report

Middle School: The Worst Years of My Life (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,007,149Details
International Box Office $3,304,144
Worldwide Box Office $23,311,293
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,541,643
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $873,490
Total $3,415,133
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Rafe has an epic imagination...and a slight problem with authority. Both collide when he transfers to an oppressive, rule-crazy middle school. Drowning in do's and don'ts, Rafe and his scheming best friend Leo hatch a plan to break every rule in the school's Code of Conduct. It's Ferris Bueller meets Home Alone as their battle with Principal Dwight explodes into chaos both real and imagined. But Dwight displays his own fiendish creativity, striking back at the rulebreakers. Meanwhile, Rafe struggles to hide his misbehavior from Jeanne, the straight-A, overachieving girl of his dreams, and at home, his mother's boyfriend -- a moochy, jack-of-no-trades named Bear -- threatens to become his stepfather.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,878,437 (34.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.91 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:85.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$8,500,000 (worldwide box office is 2.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,822 opening theaters/2,822 max. theaters, 3.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $27,663,061

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 7th, 2016 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: October 21st, 2016 (Wide), released as Ortaokul: Hayatımın En Kötü Yılları (Turkey)
December 2nd, 2016 (Wide), released as Escola: Os Piores Anos da Minha Vida (Portugal)
January 13th, 2017 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 13th, 2017 (Wide), released as Middle School: The Worst Year Of My Life (Australia)
February 24th, 2017 (Wide), released as Училището: Най-лошите ми години (Bulgaria)
Video Release: December 20th, 2016 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for rude humor throughout, language and thematic elements.
(Rating bulletin 2439 (Cert #50625), 8/24/2016)
Running Time: 92 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animated Sequences
Plot point: Bad Teachers, Bullies, Divorcée Romance, Dysfunctional Family, New Guy/Girl in School
Social setting: Middle School
Subgenre: Family Comedy
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: CBS Films, Participant Media, James Patterson Entertainment, Steve Carr
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Rafe has an epic imagination...and a slight problem with authority. Both collide when he transfers to an oppressive, rule-crazy middle school. Drowning in do's and don'ts, Rafe and his scheming best friend Leo hatch a plan to break every rule in the school's Code of Conduct. It's Ferris Bueller meets Home Alone as their battle with Principal Dwight explodes into chaos both real and imagined. But Dwight displays his own fiendish creativity, striking back at the rulebreakers. Meanwhile, Rafe struggles to hide his misbehavior from Jeanne, the straight-A, overachieving girl of his dreams, and at home, his mother's boyfriend -- a moochy, jack-of-no-trades named Bear -- threatens to become his stepfather.
